title: Stop excessive wrapping of errors
body:
Most of the builtin golang functions like os.Stat and
os.Open report errors including the file system object
path. We should not wrap these errors and put the file path
in a second time, causing stuttering of errors when they
get presented to the user.

This patch tries to cleanup a bunch of these errors.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

just want to raise a counter point to this, although I expect to be outvoted on this.  One thing about the wrapped errors is they do help point to where in the code the error is; whereas sometimes this errors are difficult to find/determine.  Particularly when many errors have the same error message.

If the error message pointed at content that would have helped find that, then I attempted to leave the content.
I also don't believe we should make the tool errors look like crap just to help us out.
Restarted the failed tests - 1 agent timeout, 1 normal timeout (ubuntu root remote tests managed to go over 90 minutes)
